backend/main.mo: Motoko actor implementing a full CRUD expense tracker with in-memory Map storage, supporting add/update/delete/query operations, filtering by category and date range, and aggregation helpers for totals and category breakdowns.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented UI features (white background, blue colour scheme, card styling, bold amounts, etc.) to their corresponding source files, components, test IDs, and confidence levels.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON dictionary providing one-line descriptions of every frontend source file, covering screens, components, hooks, utilities, constants, and styles for the expense tracker app.
frontend/ANDROID_APK.md: Guide for building and distributing the ExpenseTracker Android APK, covering environment setup, debug/release builds, installation methods, Internet Identity auth, troubleshooting, and CI/CD integration.
frontend/android/app/src/main/AndroidMan: Android manifest declaring the MainActivity with launcher intent filter, deep link support for the `expensetracker://auth` scheme, FileProvider, and INTERNET/network permissions.
frontend/android/app/src/main/java/com/e: Minimal Android MainActivity extending Capacitor's BridgeActivity with only an onCreate override, serving as the entry point for the Capacitor-wrapped web app.
frontend/android/app/src/main/res/xml/ne: Android network security config that permits cleartext traffic globally for development while enforcing HTTPS for production IC/Internet Identity domains.
frontend/android/variables.gradle: Gradle ext block defining Android SDK versions and dependency version constants (minSdk 22, targetSdk 34) used across the Android build scripts.
frontend/ARCHITECTURE.md: Documents the frontend architecture including screen structure, component hierarchy, hooks, utilities, constants, and the full backend Motoko actor interface for the student expense tracker.
frontend/capacitor.config.ts: Capacitor configuration specifying the app ID, name, web dist directory, HTTPS Android scheme, and enabling mixed content debugging for the ExpenseTracker app.
frontend/index.css: Root CSS file applying Tailwind directives and defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for the light and dark theme colour tokens (background, primary, card, chart colours, etc.).
frontend/index.html: Root HTML entry point for the Student Expense Tracker app that mounts the React app via a `div#root` and loads `src/main.tsx` as a module.
frontend/README.md: Brief README for the ExpenseTracker frontend describing it as a React/Capacitor cross-platform app with a section for web development instructions.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Defines the TanStack Router route tree with routes for Dashboard, AddExpense, ExpenseList, Calculator, and Settings, wrapped in a ThemeProvider and Toaster.
frontend/src/components/calculator/Calcu: Form component for adding a temporary expense entry (amount, category, date, note) to a calculator session, with validation and reset on submit.
Displays the list of temporary expense entries in the current calculator session, with formatted currency/date and a remove button for each entry.
frontend/src/components/charts/MonthlySp: Renders an area chart of daily spending totals over a month using Recharts, with a gradient fill and empty-state message when no data exists.
frontend/src/components/charts/WeeklySpe: Renders a bar chart of daily spending for a given week date range using Recharts, mapping each day label to its total amount.
frontend/src/components/dashboard/Catego: Displays a sorted list of expense categories with their totals and percentage progress bars, including loading skeletons and an empty state.
frontend/src/components/dashboard/Totals: Shows two summary cards for total amount spent and average per transaction, with loading skeleton states.
frontend/src/components/expenses/DeleteE: Alert dialog that confirms deletion of a specific expense, calling the delete mutation and showing success/error toasts on completion.
frontend/src/components/expenses/EditExp: A dialog component for editing an existing expense, pre-populating the ExpenseForm with current values and calling the updateExpense mutation on submit.
frontend/src/components/expenses/Expense: A filter UI component with two dropdowns for selecting a time period (week/month/all) and an expense category to filter the expense list.
A reusable form component for creating or editing an expense, with fields for amount, category, date, and optional note, including client-side validation.
Displays a sorted list of expenses in cards with edit and delete actions, showing loading skeletons and an empty state when appropriate.
The main application layout wrapper that renders a sticky header with navigation, a main content outlet, and a footer, while initializing Capacitor runtime and auth bridge on mount.
frontend/src/components/layout/PrimaryNa: A navigation bar component rendering links to Dashboard, Add Expense, Expenses, Calculator, and Settings pages, highlighting the active route.
frontend/src/constants/expenseCategories: Exports a constant array of expense category objects with value/label pairs used across category selectors in the app.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor based on the current Internet Identity, invalidating dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useExpenses.ts: Provides React Query hooks for fetching all expenses and performing create, update, and delete mutations against the ICP backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication on the Internet Computer, managing login state, identity persistence, and AuthClient lifecycle.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that sets up Tailwind directives and defines light/dark theme CSS custom properties for colors, borders, and chart palette.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/screens/AddExpenseScreen.ts: Screen component for adding a single expense, rendering an ExpenseForm and calling the createExpense mutation on submit before navigating to the expenses list.
frontend/src/screens/CalculatorScreen.ts: Screen that lets users build a session of multiple expense entries, displays a running total, and saves all entries at once via the createExpense mutation.
frontend/src/screens/DashboardScreen.tsx: Dashboard screen displaying spending totals, a category breakdown, and bar/trend charts filtered by a week or month time window tab.
frontend/src/screens/ExpenseListScreen.t: Screen that lists all expenses with time-window and category filters applied client-side to the fetched expense data.
frontend/src/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx: Settings screen allowing the user to toggle dark/light theme and displaying basic app version and platform information.
frontend/src/utils/aggregations.ts: Utility functions to compute total expense amounts and aggregate expenses grouped by day or category, returning sorted arrays.
frontend/src/utils/capacitorAuthBridge.t: Provides a Capacitor-specific authentication bridge for Internet Identity, handling deep link callbacks and opening the identity provider in the system browser for native WebView auth flows.
frontend/src/utils/capacitorRuntime.ts: Utility module for detecting and initializing the Capacitor native runtime, exposing helpers to check platform, determine if running in Capacitor, and decide whether URLs should open externally.
frontend/src/utils/dateRanges.ts: Provides date-fns-based helpers to compute week/month ranges, generate day labels for a date interval, and filter expense arrays by a given time window.
frontend/src/utils/expenseMapping.ts: Contains conversion and formatting utilities for expenses, including bigint/number amount conversions, note normalization, and currency/date formatting.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining the design system tokens—colors (oklch-based, blue scheme), border radii, box shadows, fonts, and keyframe animations—along with plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Tracks the current project state (version 3), documenting implemented UI features such as white background, blue color scheme, rounded cards, bold amounts, grey date text, and improved spacing.
